solve(Nums, Target) ->
    % Helper function to calculate XOR sum of a list
    xor_sum(List) ->
        lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) -> Acc xor X end, 0, List).

    % Helper function to generate all (XOR_sum, count) pairs for a given list
    % Returns a list of {XOR, Count} tuples.
    generate_pairs(Nums) ->
        % Recursive DFS helper
        dfs(0, 0, 0, Nums, []).

    dfs(Index, CurrentXor, CurrentCount, Nums, Accumulator) when Index == length(Nums) ->
        % Base case: reached the end, record the pair
        [{CurrentXor, CurrentCount} | Accumulator];

    dfs(Index, CurrentXor, CurrentCount, Nums, Accumulator) ->
        % Element at current index
        Element = lists:nth(Index, Nums),
        NextIndex = Index + 1,

        % 1. Exclude Element
        Acc1 = dfs(NextIndex, CurrentXor, CurrentCount, Nums, []),

        % 2. Include Element
        Acc2 = dfs(NextIndex, CurrentXor xor Element, CurrentCount + 1, Nums, []),

        % Combine results
        lists:append(Acc1, Acc2).

    % --- Main logic ---

    TotalXor = xor_sum(Nums),
    TargetPrime = TotalXor xor Target,

    N = length(Nums),
    N_A = floor(N / 2),

    % Split Nums into A and B
    A = lists:sublist(Nums, 0, N_A),
    B = lists:sublist(Nums, N_A, N - N_A),

    % Generate pairs for A and B
    PairsA = generate_pairs(A),
    PairsB = generate_pairs(B),

    % Convert pairs list to maps: XOR_sum -> Min_Count
    % This handles duplicate XOR sums by keeping the minimum count.
    build_map(Pairs) ->
        % Use maps:fold to process the list and maintain the minimum count for each key
        maps:fold(fun(Xor, Count, Map) ->
            case maps:get(Xor, Map) of
                {CurrentMinCount} when Count >= CurrentMinCount -> Map;
                _ -> maps:put(Xor, Count, Map)
            end
        end, maps:new(), Pairs).

    MapA = build_map(PairsA),
    MapB = build_map(PairsB),

    % Find the minimum combined count
    MinRemovedCount = N + 1,

    % Iterate over MapA
    min_count_finder(MapA, MapB, TargetPrime, MinRemovedCount),

    % Return result
    if MinRemovedCount > N ->
        -1;
    true ->
        MinRemovedCount
end.

% Helper function to find the minimum combined count
min_count_finder(MapA, MapB, TargetPrime, MinCount) ->
    maps:fold(fun(XorA, CountA, _Map) ->
        % Calculate required XorB
        XorB = TargetPrime xor XorA,

        case maps:get(XorB, MapB) of
            {CountB} ->
                % Found a match, calculate combined count
                CombinedCount = CountA + CountB,
                % Update MinCount if smaller
                MinCountNew = min(MinCount, CombinedCount),
                MinCountNew;
            _ ->
                % No match found for this XorA
                MinCount
        end
    end, MinCount, MapA).

generate_sums(List) ->

    InitialMap = #{0 => 0},
    
    % Fold over the elements of List
    lists:foldl(fun(CurrentMap, Element) ->
        % 1. Generate updates derived from CurrentMap
        Updates = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, {XOR_sum, Size}) ->
            New_XOR = XOR_sum xor Element,
            New_Size = Size + 1,

            CurrentBestSize = maps:get(New_XOR, Acc, 100), % Use a safe upper bound > N
            
            if New_Size < CurrentBestSize ->
                lists:insert(New_XOR-New_Size, Acc);
            true
        end, [], maps:fold(fun(K, V, _) -> {K, V} end, CurrentMap)),
        
        % 2. Merge updates back into CurrentMap. 
        % For conflicting keys, we take the minimum size.
        maps:merge(CurrentMap, Updates, fun(New, Old) -> min(New, Old) end)
    end, InitialMap, List).

-spec find_min_removed/4 -> integer().
find_min_removed(Map1, Map2, T_prime, N) ->
    % Initial minimum is N+1 (effectively infinity)
    InitialMin = N + 1,
    
    % Iterate over all pairs {X, S1} in Map1
    Min = maps:fold(fun(X, S1, Acc) ->
        Y = X xor T_prime,
        
        % Check if Y exists in Map2
        case maps:get(Y, Map2) of
            {ok, S2} ->
                NewTotalSize = S1 + S2,
                min(Acc, NewTotalSize);
            error ->
                Acc
        end
    end, InitialMin, Map1).

-spec solve([integer()], integer()) -> integer().
solve(Nums, Target) ->
    N = length(Nums),
    
    % 1. Calculate total XOR sum S
    S = lists:foldl(fun(Acc, X) -> Acc xor X end, 0, Nums),
    
    % 2. Calculate required XOR sum T' for the removed set R
    T_prime = S xor Target,
    
    % Trivial case: if T' is 0, the empty set R works, minimum removal is 0.
    if T_prime =:= 0 ->

        true
    end,

    % 3. Split Nums into two halves
    N1 = floor(N / 2),
    Nums1 = lists:sublist(Nums, 0, N1),
    Nums2 = lists:sublist(Nums, N1, N - N1),

generate_sums(Nums) ->

%% generate_sums(Nums)
%% Generates a map where keys are achievable XOR sums, and values are the maximum subset size required to achieve that sum.
generate_sums(Nums) ->
    InitialMap = #{0 => 0}.
    
    lists:foldl(fun(CurrentMap, Num) ->
        
        % 1. Calculate NewPossibilities: maps generated by including Num
        NewPossibilities = maps:fold(fun(X, S) ->
            NewX = X `xor` Num,
            NewS = S + 1,
            #{NewX => NewS}
        end, CurrentMap),

        % 2. Merge NewPossibilities into CurrentMap, prioritizing larger sizes
        maps:fold(fun(X, NewS) ->
            case maps:get(X, CurrentMap) of
                undefined -> 
                    maps:put(X, NewS, CurrentMap);
                ExistingS when NewS > ExistingS ->
                    maps:put(X, NewS, CurrentMap);
                _ -> 
                    CurrentMap % ExistingS >= NewS
            end
        end, CurrentMap)
    end, InitialMap, Nums).

%% solve(Nums, Target)
%% Returns the minimum number of removals needed to achieve Target XOR sum, or -1 if impossible.
solve(Nums, Target) ->
    N = lists:length(Nums),
    N_A = N div 2,
    
    A = lists:take(Nums, N_A),
    B = lists:sublist(Nums, N_A),
    
    MapA = generate_sums(A),
    MapB = generate_sums(B),
    
    % Initialize K_max to -1 (meaning no solution found yet)
    K_max = -1,
    
    % Iterate through MapA to find the best combination
    K_max_final = maps:fold(fun(X, K_A) ->
        Y_needed = X `xor` Target,
        
        case maps:get(Y_needed, MapB) of
            undefined -> 
                K_max % No match in MapB
            K_B -> 
                K_total = K_A + K_B,
                % Update K_max with the maximum total size found so far
                max(K_max, K_total)
        end
    end, K_max, MapA),
    
    case K_max_final of
        -1 -> -1;
        K -> N - K
    end.

                % No match in MapB for this XA
                Acc
        end
    end, -1, MapA),
    
    % 4. Determine the minimum removals
    if MaxTotalSize >= 0 ->
        N - MaxTotalSize;
    true ->
        -1
    end.
